description: Formal evaluation prompts for the nestjs-dev-guidelines skill. Each task exercises patterns where the skill's rules should change the model's output materially vs. without the skill.

tasks.prompt: In a NestJS 11 project using raw pg and Better Auth, add a `payments` module that lists a user's own payments. Support filtering by status (pending/paid/refunded/canceled) and created-at range, sorting, and cursor-based pagination. Include the DTO, service, controller, SQL, and indexes. Error shape should match the rest of the system.
expectations: Module lives at src/modules/payment/ with singular folder name
Files named kebab-case: payment.module.ts, payment.controller.ts, payment.service.ts, list-payments.query.dto.ts, payment.repository.ts
Controller versioned at /v1/payments; plural resource noun
DTO uses class-validator decorators (@IsEnum, @IsDateString, @Type, @IsInt, @Min, @Max, @IsOptional)
Global ValidationPipe assumed with whitelist + forbidNonWhitelisted
Sort field whitelisted against an allowed list
Pagination is cursor-based with a max limit (≤100)
Response is { data, meta: { pagination: { nextCursor, hasMore, limit } } }
Query parameterized (no string concatenation); uses (created_at, id) tuple compare
Composite index on (user_id, created_at DESC, id DESC) with partial WHERE deleted_at IS NULL
Service uses constructor DI; controller is thin and delegates to service
Ownership check: query is scoped to the authenticated user (WHERE user_id = $userId)
@ApiTags, @ApiOperation, @ApiResponse decorators present
anti_expectations: Uses Prisma/TypeORM/Drizzle without being asked (user specified raw pg)
Uses offset-based pagination by default
DTO typed as `any` or `object`
User-supplied sort field passed directly into SQL
Returns raw array or custom envelope like { success, result }
Float amount or missing amount_cents integer convention

expectations: Flags missing signature verification (HMAC / stripe.webhooks.constructEvent) as a BLOCKER
Flags SQL string concatenation (stripe_id interpolated into SQL) as a BLOCKER
Flags missing idempotency / replay protection (dedupe by event.id) as a BLOCKER
Flags @Body() body: any and missing DTO/Zod validation
Flags console.log — should use structured pino logger
Flags { success: true/false } envelope — should throw HttpException with code + filter writes envelope
Flags controller doing business logic directly against DB — should delegate to a service/repository
Flags non-versioned controller path (no /v1)
Cites specific reference files (e.g., 11-security.md, 14, 18, 21, 07)
Clear sections: Blockers / Suggestions / Approval-conditional verdict
anti_expectations: Approves without blockers
Only mentions style issues without security
Vague feedback like 'this could be better'

tasks.prompt: Design the Postgres schema for an AI chat app backend: users, organizations, conversations, messages with streaming partials, tool calls, and attachments. We want multi-tenant isolation, soft delete, and cursor pagination over messages in a conversation. Give the CREATE TABLE statements and the indexes.
expectations: Tables plural snake_case: users, organizations, conversations, messages, message_tool_calls, attachments
Columns snake_case; primary keys named `id` as uuid with defaults (UUIDv7 or similar)
Foreign keys named <entity>_id and indexed
ON DELETE policies stated explicitly (CASCADE for owned data like messages, RESTRICT/SET NULL for references)
All timestamp columns are timestamptz
created_at, updated_at, deleted_at columns present where applicable
Unique constraints respect soft delete via partial unique indexes (WHERE deleted_at IS NULL)
multi-tenant: conversations and related tables carry organization_id
Composite index on messages for cursor pagination: (conversation_id, created_at DESC, id DESC)
Check constraints for enums (role IN ('user','assistant','system','tool'), status)
Money / cost columns as integer (bigint) minor units if present
Attachments reference external storage (S3 key) rather than storing bytes
Tool calls captured with arguments jsonb and result/status
anti_expectations: Singular table names (user, message)
PascalCase or camelCase columns
timestamp without tz
FK without index
No multi-tenancy hint (organization_id missing)
Auto-increment integer as public id

tasks.prompt: In a NestJS 11 AI product backend, scaffold a `summarize` feature that takes long text and returns a JSON summary (title, 5 bullets, tone). It must use an LLM gateway pattern, prompt caching for the fixed system prompt, Zod-validated output, per-user usage metering, and monthly quota enforcement. Show the files and key code (not complete implementations).
expectations: Feature lives in src/modules/summarize/ with module/controller/service/DTO pattern
LLM gateway lives in src/integrations/llm/ with provider-agnostic LlmService
Feature calls this.llm.call(...) — never imports @anthropic-ai/sdk or similar directly
Zod schema defines the summary shape and is used to parse output; optional single-shot repair retry on schema mismatch
Prompt versioned (e.g., summarize-v1.md) with stable model id (no 'latest')
Anthropic cache_control: 'ephemeral' applied to the system prompt
UsageMeteringService.record called after every call, including failures
QuotaService.assertWithin called before the call; returns 429 BILLING.QUOTA_EXCEEDED when over limit
Timeout set via AbortSignal.timeout; retries with backoff; fallback model chain
Traced via Langfuse/Helicone or OTel span
DTO with class-validator on the input endpoint
Controller is thin; versioned (/v1/summarize)
anti_expectations: Direct Anthropic SDK usage in the feature service
No Zod validation (trusts JSON.parse of model output)
Hard-coded 'claude-latest' or drifting model alias
No cost tracking
No timeout or retry

tasks.prompt: Add an endpoint that exports all of a user's payments as CSV. The table may contain 100k+ rows per user. Requirements: cursor-based internally for streaming, proper Content-Disposition, no OOM, authenticated, rate-limited to avoid abuse.
expectations: Streams response (res.write in chunks or stream pipe) — does not buffer all rows in memory
Uses Postgres cursor / batched fetch (e.g., pg Cursor or keyset pagination internally)
Sets Content-Type: text/csv and Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=...
Guarded by AuthGuard; filters WHERE user_id = $currentUser
Rate-limited (throttler) more strictly than normal endpoints
Global exception filter guard: skips when res.headersSent (streaming)
Uses parameterized queries
Does not SELECT *; selects only the columns needed for CSV
Adds audit log entry for data export
anti_expectations: Loads all rows with findAll() before streaming
Default JSON envelope for a binary/CSV response
No auth / no ownership filter

tasks.prompt: Add a BullMQ worker that delivers outbound webhooks to user-configured URLs. Requirements: retry with exponential backoff, DLQ for poison jobs, idempotent delivery, HMAC signature, timeout per attempt, trace propagation. Show the producer and worker files plus the DB/schema needed.
expectations: BullMQ queue registered; producer service enqueues with attempts + backoff + jobId for dedupe
Worker marks processed to ensure idempotency (inbox table or unique constraint)
HMAC signature header (e.g., X-Signature: sha256=...) computed from raw body + per-endpoint secret
fetch / axios call has AbortSignal.timeout
Non-retryable errors (4xx) explicitly thrown as UnrecoverableError; 5xx/429/timeout retried
DLQ monitoring mentioned (bull-board or alert)
Traceparent propagated via job payload
Worker is deployed as a separate process in production
Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM
Structured logging per job (jobId, url, attempt, outcome)
anti_expectations: Fire-and-forget without retries
Infinite retry without DLQ
No dedupe / not idempotent (duplicate deliveries on retry)
No timeout on fetch

tasks.prompt: The API has inconsistent error responses. Some endpoints return `200 { success: false, error: 'bad email' }`, others throw `HttpException` with just strings, some return `500` on validation failures. Describe the standard error envelope, error code taxonomy, global filter implementation, and how class-validator errors are mapped. Show code snippets.
expectations: Envelope: { error: { code, message, details?, traceId } } with correct HTTP status
Error code taxonomy uses namespaced strings like USER.EMAIL_TAKEN, PAYMENT.INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS, VALIDATION.FAILED, AUTH.UNAUTHORIZED
Status code mapping: 400/401/403/404/409/422/429/500/502/503 with correct meanings
Global AllExceptionsFilter @Catch() registered via APP_FILTER
Filter skips write if res.headersSent (streaming / raw handlers)
class-validator errors (array of messages) mapped to VALIDATION.FAILED with details array
traceId sourced from request id / X-Request-ID header
5xx logged with stack; 4xx not spammed
No internal details (stack, query, file paths) leaked in response
anti_expectations: Continues to use 200 with success:false in the proposed standard
Uses numeric error codes without a namespace
Leaves class-validator errors as raw `message: string[]`

tasks.prompt: Our endpoints currently only check authentication. Users can access other organizations' data by guessing IDs. Add multi-tenant authorization for organizations and projects: memberships, roles (owner/admin/member), and ensure every query is scoped. Show the schema change, guard pattern, and controller/service adjustments.
expectations: memberships(user_id, organization_id, role) with unique (user_id, organization_id)
role enum constrained by CHECK or Postgres enum: ('owner', 'admin', 'member')
A guard or decorator enforces the user belongs to the resource's organization
Service-level queries include `WHERE organization_id = $currentOrg`
404 returned (not 403) when user cannot see the resource — to avoid leaking existence
Role-based check for privileged actions (admin/owner for destructive ops)
Audit log for role changes
The URL structure reflects scoping when it makes sense (/v1/organizations/:orgId/projects/:id) OR current-org derived from session
No reliance solely on the URL id — the service verifies ownership
anti_expectations: Only adding an isAdmin boolean without org membership
Checking only in the controller, not in the service / DB query
Exposing 403 vs 404 distinctions that reveal resource existence

evaluation.method: Human review or LLM-as-judge checking how many `expectations` items are satisfied in the output, and that `anti_expectations` are absent. Score per task = (met_expectations - violated_anti_expectations) / total_expectations.
notes: Run each task twice: once with this skill available, once without. The skill is valuable if with-skill scores are materially higher (>15 percentage points) or if anti-expectations are avoided more consistently.
Tasks 2 (webhook-review) and 7 (error-taxonomy-standardize) especially test review-mode and code-fluency on rules that are easy to miss without the skill.
Refresh expectations when rules evolve (keep this file in sync with references/).
